dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal describes a channel breakout strategy using n-period highest high and lowest low. Mapped to Donchian Channel indicators (dc_upper, dc_lower, dc_middle) which dynamically calculate the range over past n-periods. The lookback_period_n parameter of 20 aligns with standard Donchian implementations. Entry conditions trigger on channel breakouts; exit conditions trigger on channel reversal or opposite boundary breach. Both LONG and SHORT directions included per CONTEXT_DEPENDENT specification.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal describes a channel breakout strategy using n-period highest high and lowest low. Mapped to Donchian Channel indicators (dc_upper, dc_lower, dc_middle) which dynamically calculate the range over past n-periods. The lookback_period_n parameter of 20 aligns with standard Donchian implementations. Entry conditions trigger on channel breakouts; exit conditions trigger on channel reversal or opposite boundary breach. Both LONG and SHORT directions included per CONTEXT_DEPENDENT specification.

source_rule_text: A trader should adjust the lookback period of a channel breakout indicator based on the trend magnitude they wish to capture—larger lookback periods detect larger trends with fewer false signals, while smaller periods detect smaller trends with higher sensitivity.
